title: Molten Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and indulgent Egyptian-inspired dish featuring lamb, doenjang, and aromatic spices, all in a crispy, salty tagine.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: look for a fatty shoulder for best results

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven or clay tagine over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb and cook until browned, about 5 minutes, then remove from heat and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot, then add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, coriander, and paprika to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the browned lamb back to the pot, along with the doenjang, salt, and black pepper.
Stir to combine, then add enough water to the pot to barely cover the lamb.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the lid and increase the heat to medium-high to allow the sauce to thicken.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed, then serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro.
